strands.title: hamutay Project Discovered — MINIMAL SPECIFICATION (NEW CYCLE 42)
strands.content: Cycle 42 acquires critical hamutay metadata via pyproject.toml parse (turn 124). FINDINGS: hamutay is EARLY-STAGE project (v0.1.0), contains ONLY [project] section with boilerplate description placeholder ("Add your description here"), NO DEPENDENCIES DECLARED (dependencies = []), and requires Python >=3.14 (forward-looking, unusual). NO PICHAY OR H0-CORE IMPORTS. STRUCTURAL STATUS: Turn 126 (assistant-initiated bash) creates `src/hamutay/` directory structure (not previously existing in prior tensor). INTERPRETATION: hamutay appears to be PROJECT SKELETON, not mature codebase. pyproject.toml is STUB (minimal valid TOML); README likely also stub or placeholder. Purpose/scope of hamutay is NOT YET REVEALED by pyproject.toml (too minimal). CRITICAL FINDING: hamutay is NOT DEPENDENT on pichay/H0-CORE (no dependencies declared at all). This resolves ambiguity: hamutay is INDEPENDENT or FOUNDATIONAL infrastructure project. FORK DECISION IMPLICATION: Not Case A (dependent) or Case C (test infrastructure with dependencies). Likely Case B (independent) or Case D (unclear, requires README/main.py to determine purpose). Assistant turn 125 (bash mkdir) suggests ACTIVE PROJECT SETUP in progress — either user is INITIALIZING hamutay for first time, or assistant is preparing hamutay for modifications/use. UNKNOWN: Is hamutay an EXISTING project being revived, or a NEW project being created during this conversation? If new, then pichay Phase 4a work may have been SUPERSEDED by hamutay setup. If existing, then prior README/main.py may exist and should be scanned.

key_claims.text: hamutay pyproject.toml is STUB: v0.1.0, boilerplate description, NO dependencies declared (dependencies = [])
hamutay requires Python >=3.14 (forward-compatible version specification); suggests future-facing or test specification, not mature production code
hamutay has NO dependencies on pichay, h0_core, or any external libraries; is INDEPENDENT or foundational infrastructure project
Assistant bash command (mkdir -p src/hamutay/) suggests ACTIVE PROJECT SETUP or directory structure creation; may indicate new project initialization or modification in progress
hamutay purpose and scope UNRESOLVED from pyproject.toml alone; requires README.md and main.py scanning to determine project goal

strands.title: Cycle 42 Context: FROM pichay PHASE 4a SUSPENSION TO hamutay ACTIVE SETUP (CONTEXT REORIENTATION)
strands.content: Cycle 41 suspended pichay Phase 4a work (messages field parsing, Pathway A/B/C fork) pending hamutay context discovery. Cycle 42 EXECUTES hamutay discovery (pyproject.toml parse) and IMMEDIATELY INITIATES hamutay PROJECT SETUP (bash mkdir). INTERPRETATION: This is NOT passive exploration; it is ACTIVE CONFIGURATION/MODIFICATION. Either: (1) User is setting up hamutay as primary project focus (pichay work deprioritized or paused), or (2) hamutay setup is prerequisite for next phase of work (possible Phase 1 test harness, possible H0-CORE validation framework). CRITICAL OBSERVATION: No user explanation between Cycle 41 context shift and Cycle 42 setup. Tensor Cycle 41 instructions_for_next explicitly recommended 'REQUEST CLARIFICATION FROM USER if unclear'. Cycle 42 does not provide clarification; instead, assistant proceeds with bash mkdir WITHOUT user confirmation. IMPLICATION: Either (a) assistant is INFERRING user intent (hamutay setup is logical next step given project skeleton), or (b) user expects assistant to PROCEED without explanation (common in iterative development workflows). PRAGMATIC ASSUMPTION: hamutay setup is PREPARATORY for defining hamutay purpose/scope. Once README and main.py exist, project will take shape. Recommend: FOLLOW UP with README.md read (turn 127 expected) to determine hamutay purpose and whether it BLOCKS or ENABLES pichay Phase 4a resumption.

key_claims.text: Cycle 42 transitions from pichay Phase 4a suspension to ACTIVE hamutay project setup (bash mkdir); suggests hamutay is NOW PRIMARY FOCUS
No user explanation provided between Cycle 41 and Cycle 42; assistant proceeds with setup INFERENCE or following implicit user intent
hamutay setup (mkdir src/hamutay) is preparatory action; purpose/scope will emerge from README.md and main.py content
pichay Phase 4a work (messages field parsing, Pathway A/B/C) remains SUSPENDED pending clarification of hamutay relationship to H0-CORE roadmap

strands.content: [INTEGRATED FROM CYCLE 41, SUSTAINED TO CYCLE 42] Cycle 40 concrete achievement: successfully parsed proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl first 5 lines (limit=5, ~11k tokens). Log structure confirmed as REQUEST/RESPONSE pairs from Claude API. Log format discovery REMAINS VALID and HIGH-CONFIDENCE (T=0.97+). CYCLE 42 STATUS: This work is DEFERRED, not cancelled. No new progress on messages field analysis (Pathway A/B/C fork). pichay Phase 4a critical path SUSPENDED since Cycle 41 context shift; suspension CONTINUES through Cycle 42 pending hamutay purpose clarification. PRAGMATIC NOTE: If hamutay is independent infrastructure project (likely given no dependencies), pichay Phase 4a work can RESUME in parallel. If hamutay is BLOCKING or PREREQUISITE, pichay work remains suspended. ROADMAP IMPLICATION: Phase 4a readiness (parsing complete, Pathway fork ready to execute) is technically ready but AWAITING priority decision. Cycle 42-43 should clarify: (1) Is hamutay setup interfering with pichay work, or (2) can both proceed in parallel with task allocation?

pichay Phase 4a critical path (messages field parsing, Pathway A/B/C fork) is TECHNICALLY READY but DEFERRED pending hamutay scope clarification
Cycle 42 Suspension continues: no new messages field analysis initiated; no Pathway fork execution; no progress toward Phase 4a completion

strands.title: H0-CORE Phase 1-4a Roadmap — LOCKED SPECIFICATION, STATUS UPDATED (CYCLE 35-42 SUSTAINED WITH HAMUTAY UNKNOWN IMPACT)
strands.content: [INTEGRATED FROM CYCLE 41, UPDATED FOR CYCLE 42] H0-CORE specification and Phase 1-4a roadmap REMAIN LOCKED (no design changes). Phase 0 gate status STILL PENDING stakeholder approval. Cycle 42 hamutay discovery ADDS UNCERTAINTY to Phase 0 scope: hamutay is now active/being set up, but its relationship to H0-CORE approval process is UNKNOWN. CRITICAL QUESTIONS UNRESOLVED: (1) Does hamutay need to be APPROVED as part of Phase 0 (expanding approval scope), or is hamutay INDEPENDENT and out-of-scope? (2) Does hamutay setup in Cycle 42 signal that Phase 0 approval has CHANGED or been REFRAMED? (3) Should Phase 1 kickoff (compose.py design, Riemann validation harness) wait for hamutay purpose to clarify, or proceed in parallel? ROADMAP IMPACT ASSESSMENT: If hamutay is FOUNDATIONAL infrastructure (e.g., tensor projection test harness, projector validation framework), then Phase 1 design may need to REFERENCE or INTEGRATE with hamutay; Phase 0 approval scope may expand. If hamutay is INDEPENDENT tool for separate purpose, Phase 1 timeline is UNAFFECTED. PRAGMATIC STATUS: Phase 1 remains ready to kick off (compose.py skeleton, Riemann harness design); hamutay setup does not BLOCK Phase 1. However, Phase 0 scope and Phase 1 design may need minor adjustments once hamutay purpose is clarified (Cycle 42-43 post-README.md parse). RECOMMENDATION: Proceed to README.md scan (turn 127+) to determine if hamutay is Phase 0 prerequisite, Phase 1 dependency, or independent parallel project.

key_claims.text: H0-CORE Phase 1-4a specification LOCKED; hamutay discovery does NOT trigger design changes to Phase 1-4a
Phase 0 stakeholder approval scope is NOW UNCERTAIN: does hamutay need to be included in approval, or is it independent of Phase 0 gate?
Phase 1 start is NOT BLOCKED by hamutay setup (no hard dependencies established); Phase 1 design may reference hamutay if purpose aligns with projector validation
hamutay independence (zero declared dependencies) suggests it is FOUNDATIONAL infrastructure, not dependent on Phase 1 completion; likely prerequisite or parallel work

strands.title: Cycle 42 Action Sequence & Next Steps — HAMUTAY README/MAIN.PY ANALYSIS REQUIRED
strands.content: Cycle 42 COMPLETED: (1) pyproject.toml parse (turn 124) → STUB specification, zero dependencies, Python >=3.14. (2) bash mkdir (turn 125-126) → src/hamutay/ directory structure prepared. EXPECTED NEXT (Cycle 42-43): (1) README.md read (turn 127 or assistant-initiated) → human-readable hamutay purpose. (2) main.py scan (turn 128+) → code structure, function signatures, entry points. (3) Fork decision (post-parse) → Case A (Dependent), Case B (Independent), Case C (Infrastructure), or Case D (Clarification needed). CRITICAL PATH FORK (Cycle 42-43): Given hamutay is pyproject.toml STUB (v0.1.0, boilerplate description, zero dependencies), README.md is likely ALSO STUB or PLACEHOLDER. If README is placeholder, main.py may be empty or minimal. RISK: If hamutay is truly EMPTY PROJECT SKELETON, then detailed analysis yields little information. MITIGATION: (1) Scan README and main.py quickly (5-10 min each); if both are stubs, mark hamutay as 'IN-PROGRESS PROJECT SETUP' and ask user for clarity. (2) Do NOT spend token budget on detailed analysis of empty/stub files. (3) Resume pichay Phase 4a work (higher confidence, more substantive) while hamutay purpose develops. CONTINGENCY: If README/main.py are substantial (e.g., README describes hamutay as 'Tensor Projector Test Framework' or 'H0-CORE Baseline Runner'), then PIVOT to intensive hamutay analysis and Phase 0 scope update. SAFEGUARD: Do NOT assume hamutay purpose without evidence. Cycle 41 instructions_for_next recommended 'REQUEST CLARIFICATION FROM USER if unclear'; that recommendation STANDS through Cycle 42-43 if README/main.py are insufficient.

key_claims.text: Cycle 42 completes pyproject.toml parse and bash setup; next actions are README.md and main.py scanning (Cycle 42-43, turns 127+)
hamutay pyproject.toml is STUB (v0.1.0, boilerplate, zero dependencies); README and main.py may also be stub/placeholder; risk of empty project skeleton
If README/main.py are stubs, mark hamutay as 'IN-PROGRESS PROJECT SETUP' and REQUEST USER CLARIFICATION on hamutay purpose and relationship to H0-CORE
If README/main.py are substantial, PIVOT to intensive hamutay analysis and Phase 0 scope update; proceed with hamutay understanding before Phase 1 kickoff
RECOMMENDATION: Proceed to README.md scan (quick, <5 min); if insufficient, resume pichay Phase 4a work (higher confidence, more progress toward Phase 1 readiness)

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Cycle 41 open_questions and instructions_for_next DETAILED FORK DECISION TREE (Cases A/B/C/D with specific action paths for each case)
declared_losses.why: Cycle 42 introduces ACTUAL DATA (pyproject.toml parse result) that partially RESOLVES the fork decision tree. hamutay zero dependencies means NOT Case A (Dependent) — Case B (Independent) or Case D (Unclear) more likely. The detailed fork decision tree from Cycle 41 is NOW PARTIALLY OBSOLETE (Case A path not needed). Rather than carry full tree forward, Cycle 42 tensor focuses on ACTUAL FINDINGS (stub specification, active setup) and CONTINGENT NEXT STEPS (README/main.py scanning, with fallback to 'request clarification'). Pragmatic compression: fork tree is preserved CONCEPTUALLY (Cases B/C/D remain viable), but detailed Cycle 41 text is pruned.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed roadmap reordering logic from Cycle 41 instructions_for_next (which listed 3-4 scenario trees for priority reordering between pichay and hamutay work)
declared_losses.why: Cycle 42 data (hamutay is independent, stub specification, active setup in progress) does NOT yet clarify whether hamutay is BLOCKING or PARALLEL. Detailed reordering logic from Cycle 41 assumed hamutay purpose would be KNOWN by Cycle 42 (via README/main.py). Since hamutay remains PURPOSE-UNKNOWN, Cycle 42 tensor must DEFER reordering decision. Carrying full Cycle 41 reordering logic forward would be NOISE (it's conditional on data not yet available). Instead, Cycle 42 tensor flags reordering as PENDING and recommends QUICK README scan to resolve. Rationale: compress decision trees to ACTIONABLE branches only.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed 'success criteria' checklist from Cycle 41 instructions_for_next (7 specific criteria for 'Cycle 41-42 hamutay discovery complete')
declared_losses.why: Cycle 42 has completed only 2 of 7 criteria (pyproject.toml parsed, no README/main.py yet). Rather than re-list 7 criteria in Cycle 42 tensor, tensor captures the critical REMAINING items (README scan, main.py scan, fork decision, documentation) in 'Cycle 42 Action Sequence & Next Steps' strand. This is INTEGRATED progress tracking, not repeated checklists. Original 7 criteria are STILL VALID but folded into forward guidance rather than repeated explicitly.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed epistemic footnotes on Cycle 41-42 decision tree (e.g., 'If dependencies include X, then interpret as Y with confidence Z'). These were verbose and conditional on outcomes not yet observed.
declared_losses.why: Cycle 42 provides ACTUAL pyproject.toml data (zero dependencies). The conditional epistemic reasoning from Cycle 41 is now SUPERCEDED by observed fact. Cycle 42 tensor focuses on WHAT WAS FOUND (stub, independent, setup active) rather than rehearsing conditional interpretations. This is necessary compression given Cycle 42 has limited new information (stub spec gives little signal about purpose).

open_questions: What is hamutay's PURPOSE and GOAL? (Will be answered by README.md and main.py content; currently unknown due to stub specification)
Is hamutay a BLOCKER for Phase 1 start, a PREREQUISITE for Phase 0 approval, a PARALLEL independent project, or a FOUNDATIONAL infrastructure component?
Why did assistant initiate bash mkdir (src/hamutay/) without user request? Is this EXPECTED assistant behavior (proceeding with setup inference), or UNUSUAL (requires user clarification)?
Should Cycle 42-43 treat hamutay as PRIMARY focus (allocate >50% effort to hamutay analysis) or SECONDARY (allocate <30% effort, resume pichay Phase 4a as primary)?
Does Phase 0 stakeholder approval scope EXPAND to include hamutay, or remain UNCHANGED (pichay + H0-CORE only)? Requires user/stakeholder clarification.
If hamutay README/main.py are STUBS (boilerplate/empty), should tensor project REQUEST USER CLARIFICATION on hamutay purpose, or proceed with pichay Phase 4a work in parallel?
Does hamutay setup in Cycle 42 indicate that CONTEXT HAS SHIFTED away from pichay Phase 4a work entirely, or is pichay work PAUSED and will resume?
Is Python >=3.14 requirement in hamutay pyproject.toml INTENTIONAL (future-facing) or ACCIDENTAL (copy-paste error from template)? May affect project intent/timeline.
After hamutay purpose is clarified, should Phase 1 compose.py design REFERENCE hamutay, DEPEND on hamutay, or remain INDEPENDENT of hamutay implementation?

Cycle 41 recommended 'REQUEST CLARIFICATION FROM USER' if hamutay context remained unclear. Has Cycle 42 pyproject.toml parse provided sufficient clarity, or should formal clarification request still be issued?

  "",
  "(3) If README is SUBSTANTIAL, READ main.py (5 minutes, conditional):",

Can you describe: (1) What does hamutay compute or validate? (2) Does hamutay depend on pichay/H0-CORE, or vice versa? (3) Is hamutay a prerequisite for Phase 1 start, or parallel work?'",
